content.text:
<user_query>
Build the first operational version of a web-based global squeeze-intelligence platform. The product’s purpose is to identify emerging short-squeeze conditions across global equity markets by combining live or near-real-time market structure, regulatory short-disclosure data, filings/news catalysts, and options activity into a unified score.

Start with an MVP focused on U.S., UK, and major EU venues. Do not pretend delayed positioning data is live. Every score must show source freshness and provenance.

Tech stack: Next.js frontend, TypeScript backend/services, PostgreSQL, Redis, worker processes for ingestion/scoring, and a modular data-source adapter architecture.

Build these deliverables in order:

Monorepo scaffold
Core database schema for issuers, securities, market snapshots, positioning snapshots, catalyst events, squeeze scores, and alerts
Source adapter interfaces and mock adapters
Scoring engine v1 with explainable sub-scores
Web dashboard with overview page, regional monitor, symbol page, and alerts center
Seed/demo dataset so the product can be shown before all live feeds are connected
Branding shell: logo placeholder, color system, landing page copy, and product naming hooks

Constraints:

Keep the architecture production-minded, not hackathon-grade.
Preserve timestamps and freshness metadata for every source.
Design the system so more exchanges and vendors can be added later without rewriting the core.
Prioritize a convincing sellable demo before full global data coverage.

Output expected:

working monorepo
runnable local demo
documented setup steps
seeded example alerts
first-pass product UI that looks credible to prospects and investors
</user_query>

I’m going to scaffold a production-minded monorepo first, then implement the data model, adapters, scoring pipeline, and a credible Next.js demo UI with seeded data and alerts so you can run it locally end-to-end. I’ll start by checking the current workspace state and then create the project structure.

I found the workspace appears empty, so I’m creating the full MVP codebase from scratch now (monorepo, DB schema, adapters, scoring worker, and Next.js dashboard), then I’ll wire a seed dataset and docs so you can run a demo immediately.

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\Users\elijx\.cursor\projects\empty-window\package.json
+{
+  "name": "squeeze-intelligence-platform",
+  "private": true,
+  "version": "0.1.0",
+  "workspaces": [
+    "apps/*",
+    "packages/*"
+  ],
+  "scripts": {
+    "dev": "npm run dev --workspace @squeeze/web",
+    "build": "npm run build --workspace @squeeze/web",
+    "start": "npm run start --workspace @squeeze/web",
+    "db:up": "docker compose up -d postgres redis",
+    "db:down": "docker compose down",
+    "db:migrate": "npm run migrate --workspace @squeeze/db",
+    "db:seed": "npm run seed --workspace @squeeze/db",
+    "worker:run": "npm run run --workspace @squeeze/worker"
+  }
+}
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\Users\elijx\.cursor\projects\empty-window\tsconfig.base.json
+{
+  "compilerOptions": {
+    "target": "ES2021",
+    "module": "CommonJS",
+    "moduleResolution": "Node",
+    "strict": true,
+    "esModuleInterop": true,
+    "resolveJsonModule": true,
+    "skipLibCheck": true,
+    "declaration": true,
+    "outDir": "dist"
+  }
+}
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\Users\elijx\.cursor\projects\empty-window\docker-compose.yml
+services:
+  postgres:
+    image: postgres:16
+    container_name: squeeze-postgres
+    environment:
+      POSTGRES_USER: squeeze
+      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: squeeze
+      POSTGRES_DB: squeezeintel
+    ports:
+      - "5432:5432"
+    volumes:
+      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
+
+  redis:
+    image: redis:7
+    container_name: squeeze-redis
+    ports:
+      - "6379:6379"
+    volumes:
+      - redisdata:/data
+
+volumes:
+  pgdata:
+  redisdata:
*** End Patch

content.text: I’ve laid down the root monorepo and infra scaffolding. Next I’m adding the shared domain package and database package (schema + migration + seed pipeline), then wiring worker and UI routes to those models.
